name: rust-forge-skill
description: Production-grade Rust project scaffolding, validation, and refactoring for AI coding agents. Enforces idiomatic Rust patterns, layered architecture, thiserror/anyhow error models, and zero-tolerance quality gates.

guides.purpose: Layer architecture, module design, dependency rules
thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications
Async runtime, Axum handlers, graceful shutdown
CLI argument parsing, clap, tracing setup
Release pipeline, CI setup, MSRV policy
Forbidden patterns, code smells, zero-tolerance rules

scripts.name: validate_rust_project.sh
scripts.purpose: Full quality gate: fmt, clippy, test, audit, deny

scripts.name: audit_unsafe.sh
scripts.purpose: Find unsafe blocks, verify SAFETY comments

scripts.name: check_msrv.sh
scripts.purpose: Verify MSRV declared in Cargo.toml matches reality

capabilities.description: Scaffold a new Rust project from a template. Supports CLI, REST API, library, FFI, WASM, and workspace types.
Audit an existing Rust project against rust-forge-skill quality standards. Checks anti-patterns, errors, async, deps, tests, unsafe.
Refactor a Rust library or application to use idiomatic error handling: thiserror for domain, anyhow for application boundary.
Scaffold or audit an async Rust service using Axum + Tokio with proper layered architecture.
Audit unsafe code and FFI boundaries in a Rust FFI or WASM project.
Run a full security validation: cargo audit, cargo deny, unsafe audit, and dependency review.

deny.command: cargo deny check advisories licenses ban

expected_outputs.summary: 3–5 sentence description of what was done
expected_outputs.files_changed: Table: path, change type, why changed
expected_outputs.commands_run: List of validation commands with their output
expected_outputs.risks: List of known risks or areas with limited coverage
expected_outputs.next_steps: Actionable next steps for the user or next agent

safety_invariants.description: Agents must not delete or overwrite files outside the target project directory without explicit user permission
No unsafe block may be introduced without a SAFETY comment explaining invariant, why holds, and UB consequences
Agents must not add dependencies without confirming they are necessary and non-transitive
No API keys, tokens, or passwords may appear in logs, stdout, or source code
Agents must run all validation commands and report actual output; no simulated or truncated test results
Agents must not declare a project "ready" without running the full quality gate
